claim_boundary: This packet is unsigned preparation evidence and is not owner acceptance or cryptographic ratification.
Recommendations are advisory; every owner selection remains explicitly unselected.
All 38 objective definitions are listed, but all 38 implementation measurements remain open.
No public key, private key, credential, recovery secret, hardware-key stub, signature, tag, or publication receipt is included.
The packet does not authorize Codex to merge, sign, publish, change governance, probe privileged hardware, load a driver, modify firmware, or write physical media.
Architecture disposition does not prove PooleBoot, PooleKernel, native services, PooleGlass, PGB2, PGVM2, PDC backends, or a production ISO.

pending_owner_actions.description: Explicitly accept, amend, or reject the candidate Workstation v1 profile and all 38 target values; definition acceptance must not claim measurement evidence.
Explicitly accept, amend, or reject ADR-0003 and ADR-0004; an amendment or rejection requires a revised or superseding ADR before signing.
Choose an allowed owner-controlled SSH governance-key profile and record its public key and fingerprint without publishing private material.
Sign the canonical manifest with the PooleOS ADR namespace and verify it against the public allowed-signers and revocation files.
Create the non-replaceable owner-signed annotated architecture baseline tag over the revision carrying the manifest and detached signature.
Publish the exact main revision and signed tag, verify remote object identities, and retain the machine receipt.

adr_0003.plain_language_summary: Use Rust 2024 no_std for PooleBoot, PooleKernel, privileged services, and drivers; keep assembly minimal; use freestanding C17 for portable PDC and ABI probes; and keep Python outside production images as a host oracle and harness.
adr_0003.question: Adopt the proposed implementation-language and toolchain split?
recommendation_basis: The split reduces memory-safety exposure in privileged code while keeping explicit stable wire and disk ABIs.
The one-host Rust and LLD qualification already exercises the selected PE32+ and ELF64 target families without claiming a functional boot.
C17 remains available for independent portable PDC and ABI checks, and Python cannot become a production-image dependency.

source.source_ratification: owner acceptance and full two-host toolchain qualification pending
source.title: Implementation Language and Toolchain Split

structured_snapshot.host_evidence: Python 3 allowed as a non-production oracle and harness
structured_snapshot.pooleboot: Rust 2024 no_std on x86_64-unknown-uefi with efiapi and rust-lld
structured_snapshot.poolekernel: Rust 2024 no_std on x86_64-unknown-none ELF64 with bounded x86_64 assembly
structured_snapshot.privileged_user_space: Rust 2024 no_std with controlled alloc and generated stable ABIs

tradeoffs: Rust, LLVM, LLD, core, alloc, and compiler builtins become reviewed external build inputs.
Unsafe Rust, assembly, generated bindings, and C interoperability require continuing inventory and independent ABI fixtures.
A later language change requires a reviewed superseding ADR and new toolchain evidence.

adr_0004.plain_language_summary: Keep PooleOS component names coherent while versioning boot, kernel, syscall, IPC, driver, filesystem, package, update, recovery, desktop, receipt, crash, PGB2, and PGVM2 contracts independently.
adr_0004.question: Adopt the proposed product names and independent version namespaces?
recommendation_basis: Independent namespaces prevent an unrelated product version from silently changing wire, disk, recovery, or executable compatibility.
The names match the native architecture constitution and the current PooleGlyph PGB2 and PGVM2 integration boundary.
Unknown major versions fail closed and persistent layouts never use the unstable native Rust ABI.

source.source_ratification: owner acceptance and trademark review pending
source.title: Product Names and Version Namespaces

tradeoffs: Each public namespace needs its own compatibility, migration, test-corpus, and deprecation policy.
Renaming a namespace after publication requires explicit migration and compatibility handling.
PGB2 and PGVM2 remain unfrozen until PooleGlyph Phase 66 and later N34 evidence pass.

custody.question: Which owner-controlled governance-key profile will protect architecture ratification?
recommendation_basis: The recommended profile keeps private signing operations hardware-backed and owner-presence gated.
A passphrase Ed25519 software key is an explicit provisional fallback and requires separate risk acceptance.
Governance signing remains separate from future Secure Boot, package, update, recovery, and ISO release keys.

objectives.plain_language_summary: Freeze a measurable workstation release profile across reliability, accessibility, compatibility, privacy, and performance without claiming that any target has been measured or met.
objectives.question: Adopt the candidate PooleOS Workstation v1 profile and all 38 target definitions?
recommendation_basis: The targets are explicit, testable, evidence-bound, and fail closed instead of relying on broad quality claims.
Acceptance freezes definitions only; all 38 implementation measurements remain open.
Future evidence may justify a reviewed amendment, but measurements must not be weakened after results are observed merely to manufacture a pass.

targets.evidence_requirement: Retain every seed, launch manifest, serial log, exit reason, and failed boot.
Bind every cycle to exact hardware, firmware, media, power source, and stage markers.
Run a declared seven-day mixed workload with retained faults, resource trends, and watchdog events.
Inject interruption at every declared update transition and prove previous-known-good or recovery availability.
Check acknowledged-data, metadata, replay, repair, and mount invariants after each cut.
Exercise process death, reset, revocation, stale completion, DMA teardown, and supervised restart.
Measure from declared unrecoverable normal-slot failure to usable offline recovery UI and data-repair entry point.
Publish an applicability crosswalk; native-software exceptions require an explicit rationale and equivalent test.
Include login, permissions, settings, update, rollback, diagnostics, backup, restore, and shutdown workflows.
Verify through an independent accessibility-tree inspector and representative assistive client.
Reject clipped, overlapped, hidden, unreachable, or semantically truncated required content.
Also require 3:1 for large text and essential non-text boundaries in every required theme.
Disable nonessential animation and preserve static progress and error state without timing dependence.
Analyze every animation, transition, warning, and error effect including failure fallbacks.
Publish screen-reader and braille boundaries; graphical failure must retain keyboard and serial/GOP operation.
Advertise only exact tested firmware, device, media, and feature combinations.
Exercise older, newer, malformed, colliding, and downgrade version identifiers independently.
Bind each public contract to a declared compatibility window and retained previous-version corpus.
Keep POSIX as an explicitly enumerated source-level subset; unimplemented calls are not advertised.
Release-tree and documentation scans must reject Linux ABI, module, or driver promotion claims.
Windows remains a development host and is not a production ABI or driver source.
Inspect every shipped profile, first-run path, update, recovery, and diagnostic mode offline.
Retain packet captures for clean install, first boot, login, update UI, diagnostics, and recovery.
Test logs, network protocols, crash paths, package operations, and support exports for correlation identifiers.
Run seeded-secret and seeded-identifier corpora through preview, redaction, archive, and transport paths.
Consent must be specific, reversible, non-coercive, and independently auditable.
Verify quota, expiry, clock-change, low-space, backup, restore, and user-clear behavior.
Crash artifacts remain local until a per-export preview and explicit user action.
Measure from UEFI image entry, excluding firmware time, with serial as the independent clocked marker.
Use signed stage markers and retain timeout, dependency, driver, and fallback state.
Measure cold and warm populations separately and include software-rendered fallback.
Use representative window, text, transparency, input, and notification workloads with raw frame times.
Report animation, reduced-motion, reduced-transparency, and software-rendered populations separately.
Bind physical input timestamp, event delivery, application response, composition, and scanout observation.
Include kernel, services, drivers, compositor, caches, and retained diagnostic memory after a declared quiet period.
Measure one-second samples with network disconnected and scheduled maintenance separately declared.
Bind external instrumentation, ambient conditions, firmware, device state, display state, and workload; never override hard thermal limits.
Retain raw distributions and report cross-core, loaded, timeout, cancellation, and priority-inversion cases separately.

tradeoffs: The reliability and fault-injection sample counts require substantial lab and automation time.
Accessibility and privacy targets apply to installer and recovery paths, not only the normal desktop.
Performance gates require retained raw distributions and exact hardware, firmware, workload, and clock bindings.

public_key_publication.question: After independently reviewing the generated fingerprint, may Codex publish only the public key and fingerprint to the repository and GitHub signing-key registry?

fields.consistency_rule: yes is required only for passphrase_ed25519_provisional; hardware profiles require not_applicable

understanding_confirmations: Definition acceptance does not accept measurements or claim that PooleOS meets any target.
This response does not authorize key generation, signing, merging, tagging, or publication.
Any amendment or rejection stops manifest preparation until revised sources are reviewed again.
